textPlain: Would have been good if the license doesn't allow training in commercial LLMs :/

I’m no lawyer but I feel like clause seven leaves a clear opening to undermine the spirit of this license:> “The User may not sell this Work directly, unless they/she/he/it/ey/fae/ze/bun/puppy/foxxo … use it only as a small part of a work of a much greater scale.”That said, TIL that there are two things that can be considered “Belgium denialism” and surprisingly neither of them involves refusing to acknowledge that Belgium exists.
Wow! Had a good laugh here and there. I like the writing style ;)

"Orange Website commenters" got me, I laughed, thank you!The content, the design and the license are a nice blast from the past. Weird, cringy and highly relatable. I like the forklift certified license!

Someone once compared the web to the wild west. A set of anarchist communities getting on mostly well with no rules, then the whities discover it and introduce property and ownership. Capitalism then destroyed what was there and introduced the idea of every man for himself, which means there is no going back. FOS isn't the enemy, and it's not dead. It has just gone somewhere else and they're not telling you where.
You won't make any friends giving a gift that is equally valuable to every person in the worldTo make friends, lift some people up while putting others down.
This is now my favorite page on the internet.What a massive breath of fresh air.Just yay.
Belgium exists, but it's just a front for the waffle dealers.
If you didn't click any links, I recommend you go back and do so.
Yeah, but to be honest the anarchist communities were (and are) pretty pale and hadn't much sun ;-)
